titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the fire insurance policy for 113 Carroll Street 1990.010
titlestmt.author: Matthew Gorham
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-21 17:27:58 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

change.item: Revised by Dee Bowers to reflect rehousing

did.unittitle: Fire insurance policy for 113 Carroll Street
did.abstract: A fire insurance certificate for a building located at 113 Carroll Street in Brooklyn, dated 1890.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection consists of a single document.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Fire insurance policy for 113 Carroll Street, 1990.010, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was rehoused and the finding aid revised by Dee Bowers in 2026.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Montauk Fire Insurance Company insurance policy to Christopher Jacobson (1977.512)
Williamsburgh City Fire Insurance Company policy to Theodore F. Jackson (1978.090)
Fire insurance company letterheads in the Brooklyn letterhead collection (BCMS.0008)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A fire insurance certificate for a residence located at 113 Carroll Street in Brooklyn, dated 1890 and issued to the Estate of Emily J. Kiernan, deceased, and Marion Stuart, wife of Lebon C. Stuart, by the Guardian Assurance Company in the amount of $3400.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This item is in the public domain. While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.

controlaccess.subject: Real property -- New York (State) -- Kings County

Fire insurance -- New York (State) -- Kings County
